name: MCP server
when: User has Claude Desktop, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, or any MCP client.
note: No Rust needed. Search, browse, and dashboard tools work with zero setup. Recording and transcription need the CLI binary on PATH or to be auto-installed on first use.
activation: Best for agent-first search, recall, and meeting-memory workflows.

name: CLI
when: User wants terminal-first recording, search, import, and vault sync.
install: brew tap silverstein/tap && brew install minutes
note: Also available via cargo install minutes-cli. Requires Rust + cmake for source builds. Linux needs libasound2-dev and libpipewire-0.3-dev.
activation: Best for local operator workflows and scripting.

name: Claude Code plugin
when: User works in Claude Code and wants meeting lifecycle skills and proactive hooks.
note: 19 skills (brief, prep, debrief, graph, video review, and the rest of the meeting lifecycle) plus a meeting-analyst agent and SessionStart/PostToolUse hooks.
activation: Best for workflow guidance, prep, debrief, and meeting coaching.

name: Pi coding agent
when: User works in Pi or wants Minutes to use pi as an opt-in local summarization agent.
note: Pi auto-discovers the existing .agents/skills/minutes skill pack. Set [summarization] engine = "agent" and agent_command = "pi" to use it for post-recording summaries.
activation: Pi users who want the same local meeting memory without a Pi-specific skill export.

name: Desktop app
when: User wants a menu bar app with one-click recording, Recall, and artifact drafting.
note: Tauri v2, macOS only today. Includes command palette, dictation hotkey, live mode toggle, and auto-updates from GitHub Releases.
activation: Best for first recording, live capture, and post-meeting artifact work.
